204.reason: [When encountering a single '&', it should not be treated as AND token; this incorrectly adds an invalid token instead of skipping or handling it properly. Also, column number may be inaccurate because newline tracking is not updated during tokenization of symbols.]

reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-2: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  [Incorrectly assigns Type.AND to a single '&', which is invalid; should not produce a token but only log an error]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-3: 0.5 , reason:  [When encountering a single '&', it should not be treated as AND token; this line incorrectly adds an AND token for a lexical error case, which violates the specification that erroneous programs should only output error info and not generate invalid tokens. Additionally, the column number may be inaccurate because newline tracking is not updated during tokenization after comment removal.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-4: 0.5 , reason:  [When encountering a single '&', it should not be treated as a valid AND token; instead, only the error should be recorded without adding an invalid token]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-5: 0.5 , reason:  [Incorrectly assigns Type.AND to a single '&', which is invalid; should not produce a token after logging error 'a']

222.reason: [Same issue as above: single '|' is incorrectly emitted as OR token, which is invalid. Additionally, the lexer continues processing instead of stopping or skipping the erroneous character appropriately.]

reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-3: 0.5 , reason:  [Same issue as above: a single '|' is an error and should not produce an OR token. The lexer should avoid generating tokens for invalid symbols and only log the error.]

260.reason: [Falls back to checkKeyword for unknown symbols, but single '&' or '|' are not keywords and should not be tokenized as valid tokens; this line may also generate invalid tokens for other unexpected characters]
